pi_items.source: task.json description (line 5): 'Label: 'Urban Electronics Orders''. Pattern label must contain this text.
task.json description (line 5): 'It requires the prefix 'UE-''. Example pattern given: 'UE-2025-000001'.
task.json description (line 5): 'Padding: 6 digits'. Example pattern 'UE-2025-000001' demonstrates 6-digit padding (000001).

task.json description (line 5): 'Type: Yearly (the sequence should reset every year)'. Verified via web search: Drupal Commerce supports yearly, monthly, and infinite sequence plugins as built-in options. See: https://www.centarro.io/blog/custom-order-number-patterns-drupal-commerce

task.json description (line 5): 'Target entity type: Order'. Verified via web search and Drupal API: the order entity in Drupal Commerce is identified with machine name 'commerce_order'. See: https://docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/developer-guide/orders/getting-started/

privileged_info_summary: This task requires configuring a Drupal Commerce number pattern with the label 'Urban Electronics Orders' using the 'yearly' plugin type. The pattern must generate order numbers with prefix 'UE-', 4-digit year, hyphen, and 6-digit padding (e.g., 'UE-2025-000001'). The pattern targets the 'commerce_order' entity type and must be assigned to the 'Default' order type. All metadata values are correctly specified and correspond to actual Drupal Commerce features: 'yearly' is a built-in plugin for resetting sequences annually, and 'commerce_order' is the standard entity type ID for orders in Drupal Commerce.
